lines.en_lit: Now you are certainly impure, as it is written “Her impurity was in her skirts” said to him: Come see what is written about “That dwells with them in the midst of their impurity” even when they are impure, the Divine Presence dwells among them.
And does a matter derived by juxtaposition, again teach by juxtaposition?
this is from that and something else is not a juxtaposition
It works out well according to the one who said is not a juxtaposition but according to the one who says is a juxtaposition, what can be said?
It is locations that are derived from one another.
If you wish, say outside is derived from inside all at once,

taught: When sprinkles he does not sprinkle on the curtain but opposite the curtain. Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Yosei, said: I saw in Rome. And on were several drops of blood from the bull and the goat of Yom Kippur.
But Perhaps were bull for an unwitting communal sin the goats for idolatry,

that he saw that these were performed in their order, And we also learned a case like this with regard to bull for an unwitting communal sin: When he sprinkles, would not touch the curtain, but if it did touch, it touched,

Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Yosei, said: I saw in Rome, and there were several drops of blood on it from bull for an unwitting communal sin and goats for idolatry. But perhaps they from the bull and goat of Yom Kippur? He saw that they were performed not in their order
the blood became mixed with the blood Rava said: present once upward and seven downward, and that counts toward this and that

They said this before Rabbi Yirmeya he said: Foolish Babylonians! Because they live in a dark, land, they speak darkened halakhot, he presents upward of the goat before downward bull; and the Torah said: “And when he has finished atoning for the sacred place” He finishes the blood of the bull and afterward he finishes the blood of the goat.
Rather, Rabbi Yirmeya said He presents once upward and seven downward for the purpose of the bull, And he again presents once upward and seven downward for the purpose of the goat.

the blood became mixed with the blood during the final presentations Rav Pappa, before Rava, thought to say: He presents seven downward for the purpose of the bull and for the purpose of the goat, and he again presents once upward for the purpose of the goat.

Rava said to him: Until now they called us foolish, and now foolish of the foolish, as we teach them and they do not learn. But he presents downward for the goat before upward for the goat, and the Torah said: Present upward and then downward.
